title: feat: kube play remote build best effort
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md

```release-note
Best effort support for kube play build on remote machine
```

Following discussion this is an example of a  _Best effort_ solution from https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14527#issuecomment-2346201125

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *axel7083* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign [tomsweeneyredhat](https://github.com/tomsweeneyredhat) for approval. For more information see [the Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> Sending host paths via the API is really good for these things. That just doesn't work in actual remote scenarios.

I guess you meant is not really good ? 🤔 

Yeah, I guess the other solution is more generic, I can close this PR if the other solution is the way you are preferring

> I guess you meant is not really good ? 🤔

yes, sorry.

I think sending the full proper tarball like on build is the way to go because that works for all remote cases and not juts when the paths happens to be mounted din podman machine

> I think sending the full proper tarball like on build is the way to go because that works for all remote cases and not juts when the paths happens to be mounted din podman machine

You are right, the solution here was _best effort_ so on some machines, not all 👍 

My only concern is the speed/caching, by providing the host path, we would be able to save some time creating the tarball, and podman would probably have done just like on linux: only rebuild what changed.

I was discussing with some people using compose (while trying to convince them to use kube play!), and one remark I was getting is _how fast_ you can refresh and iterate, with feature such as `compose watch`. But this is out of the subject here, and maybe be a topic for future improvement.

Thanks for the response, closing it ! Hope https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/24435 will be merged soon 🤞
